/goal Build, verify, publish, and—only after the user releases the submission hold—submit the selected Reversible Name Atlas product profile defined by `docs/build/BUILD_SPEC.md`, following the sole vertical and fallback sequence in `docs/build/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`, until the complete Build Week submission is confirmed.

## Activation and immutable history

This amended goal is inactive merely by existing, being committed, being
reproduced in a handoff, or through implementation of the meta-plan that authored
it. It becomes binding only when the user explicitly activates this complete
text in the current primary Codex task. Record that Europe/Oslo timestamp as
C+0.

Preserve:

- historical H+0: Friday 17 July 2026 at 17:16:25 CEST;
- historical R+0: Saturday 18 July 2026 at 00:51:51 CEST;
- historical A+0: Saturday 18 July 2026 at 15:37:55 CEST;
- public known-good predecessor:
  `4baec1ed7b8553775527e3be506edab584b2b8b3`;
- completed immutable A3 fallback:
  `e3803d26d342f5c128f4e9876a7b7e35c35bde3c`;
- branch: `revision/ai-first-folder-refactor`;
- governance baseline locator: the unique commit on that branch with parent
  `e3803d26d342f5c128f4e9876a7b7e35c35bde3c` and subject
  `docs: establish connected-change operating scaffold`; use the exact SHA
  reported in the scaffold handoff and fresh Git, never a self-referential SHA.

The preceding AI-first goal was active through completed A3 and is superseded for
all future execution. Do not reset or conflate H+0, R+0, A+0, and C+0.

Absolute boundaries:

- feature freeze: Monday 20 July 2026 at 14:00 CEST;
- release candidate: Monday 20 July 2026 at 20:00 CEST;
- recording ready: Tuesday 21 July 2026 at 02:00 CEST;
- submission deadline: Wednesday 22 July 2026 at 02:00 CEST.

Targets force early integration, simplification, and optional cuts. They are not
automatic cancellation timers and never qualify incomplete work.

## Controlling authority

Use this order:

1. current user instruction;
2. this activated goal for authority and persistence;
3. frozen `docs/build/BUILD_SPEC.md` for product truth, profiles, and claims;
4. sole `docs/build/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` for dependency order, targets,
   evidence, profile status, and cuts;
5. short `docs/build/STATE.md` for observed current state and next operation;
6. fresh repository, job, Change File, match report, result, receipt, verifier,
   reconstruction, test, browser, MCP, and installed-plugin evidence for facts;
7. `docs/build/DECISIONS.md` and `docs/PREEXISTING_WORK.md` for rationale and
   provenance only.

Keep this goal and the specification frozen after activation except for a user
instruction, official-rule change, or demonstrated material defect. Update only
the sole plan and short state as evidence changes. This current task is the sole
primary integrator and must contain the majority of core development for
`/feedback`. Use subagents only for bounded non-overlapping work or independent
review and verify every material result.

Never create a second implementation branch, architecture, planner, job
authority, budget/idempotency ledger, receipt, verifier, reconstruction engine,
implementation plan, goal, state authority, or user-facing legacy workflow. The
eventual accepted fast-forward to `main` is promotion, not another implementation
branch.

## Execution loop beginning only at C+0

1. Verify the governance commit, current branch, clean state, immutable A3 and
   public/historical refs, official boundaries, and actual remaining time.
   Calculate the C targets; set C0 in progress; update `STATE.md`.
2. Run C0 before native-picker, final UI, provider, budget migration, MCP, or
   plugin work. Implement and semantically verify the complete two-layout
   transaction and refusal matrix, not only matcher units.
3. Allow the initial C0 attempt and at most two material corrections. End C0 in
   exactly one profile:
   - `CONNECTED_CHANGE_GO` only when the complete positive and negative gate
     passes; or
   - `A3_RELEASE_FALLBACK` when the same material premise survives correction 2
     or the latest C0 start time has already passed.
4. Keep only the selected profile active. Preserve failed work and evidence in
   ordinary Git history; do not reset, rebase, force-push, destructively discard,
   or retain cut claims.
5. Select the next dependency-ready vertical outcome from the sole plan.
   Implement the smallest complete user-visible path while keeping the product
   runnable.
6. Integrate focused tests, relevant full suites, visual checks, product-native
   proof, downstream-consumer checks, and stabilized documentation. Exit zero,
   code volume, unit tests alone, elapsed time, or an agent message is not
   semantic completion.
7. Record observed status/evidence in the sole plan and short state, make one
   coherent ordinary commit, and continue.
8. When a target slips, diagnose the root cause, choose the simplest route to the
   same required outcome, apply the next authorized optional cut, restore a
   runnable product, and continue. After two serious failed corrections of a
   non-C0 defect, simplify or replace the abstraction rather than apply a third
   symptom patch.
9. Under `CONNECTED_CHANGE_GO`, complete required shared MCP before adjudicating
   the optional Codex plugin once as `GO` or
   `CUT_BY_PREAUTHORIZED_GATE`. Do no plugin work before `GO`.
10. Freeze at the absolute boundary. After freeze, allow only defects, proof
    integrity, accessibility, visual QA, clean installation, documentation,
    screenshots, claims, rehearsal, packaging, and release work.
11. Continue through clean release, recording readiness, video preparation, and
    Devpost drafting. Final submission remains held until explicit user release.

Do not reopen product discovery, conduct another candidate search/tournament,
build a benchmark or validation harness, or validate validation machinery. Use
the actual product fixtures, job, Change File, matcher, receipts, verifier,
reconstruction, browser, MCP, clean clone, and normal tests.

## Authorization beginning only at C+0

Within this repository and project, activation authorizes:

- edit product, tests, fixtures, UI, documentation, packaging, and release files
  required by the selected specification profile and sole plan;
- install and package required dependencies, Blueprint assets, stable MCP v1,
  and—only after plugin `GO`—the official plugin surface;
- run local servers, browser/native-picker QA, tests, builds, MCP processes,
  clean-clone checks, and product-native jobs, Change Files, results, receipts,
  verifier outputs, and reconstructions;
- create ordinary commits and normally push the current revision branch;
- update the public repository and, only after release acceptance, fast-forward
  the accepted branch to `main` without rebase or force-push;
- make exact `gpt-5.6` Responses API calls within the existing cumulative USD
  10 cap and sole budget ledger, but only after C0 and contract stabilization;
- atomically migrate that sole ledger as specified without resetting history or
  creating a second ledger;
- if necessary, create, use, and revoke one narrowly scoped restricted project
  key through the previously authorized OpenAI Platform access, keep it only in
  local credential surfaces/environment, never expose or commit it, and make no
  payment, billing, ownership, organization, or unrelated-key change;
- prepare the public English YouTube video and update/upload the scoped video;
- create and update the Devpost draft and all submission materials;
- after explicit submission-hold release only, submit and verify receipt.

No redundant confirmation is required for those enumerated operations after
activation, except the user-owned actions and submission hold below.

Never authorize:

- secrets in chat, source, logs, tests, fixtures, replays, receipts, screenshots,
  commits, Change Files, MCP traffic, or artifacts;
- spending above USD 10 or paid cloud infrastructure;
- force-push, rebase, history rewrite, destructive source/result/Change File
  handling, or destructive treatment of unrelated or non-regenerable data;
- unrelated personal files, accounts, services, messages, or commitments;
- arbitrary filesystem/shell MCP authority, unsupported claims, or personal
  attestations Codex cannot verify;
- a second active profile, branch, engine, scaffold, or release story;
- product discovery, general semantic matching, AI-data adapters, NER/ReFinED,
  hosted service, native wrapper, React/Vite, or validation bureaucracy;
- final Devpost submission while the hold remains active.

Login recovery, MFA, CAPTCHA, payment/account changes, personal attestations,
the user's voice recording, `/feedback`, and explicit submission-hold release
remain user-owned. Request only the narrow action and continue independent work
where possible.

## Submission hold

Final Devpost submission is prohibited until the selected product profile,
renewed release materials, public video, `/feedback`, due diligence, and
submission package are complete and the user explicitly releases the hold.
Implementation, feature freeze, public-repository updates, video preparation or
upload, and Devpost drafting do not release it.

When every independently actionable requirement is complete but the hold remains
active, set `STATE.md` to `WAITING_FOR_SUBMISSION_HOLD_RELEASE`, request only
the release, and preserve the accepted release unchanged. Only after explicit
release may final submission occur. Continue until receipt/confirmation is
verified.

## Compaction recovery

After compaction, read in order:

1. this activated goal;
2. frozen specification;
3. sole implementation plan;
4. latest short state and recovery capsule;
5. material decisions and provenance;
6. fresh Git status, log, and diff;
7. actual current job, Change File, match report, accepted plan, result, receipt,
   verifier, reconstruction, tests, browser, MCP, and plugin evidence.

Matching attachment certificates can substitute for rereading only while hashes
match and the earlier reads were complete. Reread through EOF when a hash
changes, no valid certificate exists, an earlier read was incomplete, exact
wording controls the immediate decision, or evidence conflicts.

Fail closed: never infer present state from memory over fresh evidence, treat a
summary as underlying evidence, claim unread material was read, or qualify a
requirement from truncated output. Do not create a recovery ledger or custom
validation system.

## Terminal conditions

`RECORDING_READY` requires the complete selected profile, final clean clone,
final live/replay evidence, applicable origin/receiver/fallback transactions,
receipts, verifier, refusal matrix, reconstruction, stable browser, required MCP
under Connected Change, admitted plugin only if fully passed, refreshed release
materials, strictly-under-three-minute rehearsed story, timed narration and shot
list, public repository, no planned product/design work, and active submission
hold.

`WAITING_FOR_SUBMISSION_HOLD_RELEASE` requires every independent product, video,
due-diligence, `/feedback`, and Devpost-draft requirement complete while final
submission remains held.

`SUBMISSION_CONFIRMED` requires explicit hold release, final submission, and
verified receipt/confirmation. Do not mark the goal complete before that state.
